prompt: Our ingest-job status poller works great in a quick demo and then rots in production: after some minutes of polling, every request from the shared client starts failing and the process has to be restarted. We finally reproduced it deterministically against the loopback transport fake in the test suite — script a couple of 503/429 responses ahead of the 200 and Poll dies with `poll http://ingest.local/jobs/7: transport stalled: connection pool exhausted by unclosed response bodies` instead of returning the final status. The transport fake also says even our happy-path connections come back unusable. The contract for Poll hasn't changed: keep retrying 429/503 up to MaxAttempts, surface any other non-200 as a *StatusError carrying the service's structured error message verbatim (operators grep for it), report malformed 200 bodies as decode errors, propagate context cancellation so errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) works, and on EVERY one of those paths the response body has to be read to EOF and closed so the transport can reuse the connection. Fix poller.go only — poller_test.go is the contract and stays untouched. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0. repro_cmd on the baseline: TestRetryableStatusesEventuallySucceed fails with 'transport stalled: connection pool exhausted by unclosed response bodies' because the 503/429 continue path never closes bodies; the success, malformed-body, terminal-status, and budget-exhausted tests all fail requireBodiesFinished because their paths leak or fail to drain the body.
